title: From Indirect Parasite Subsidy to Direct Contribution

current_reality.situation: On welfare, disability, unemployment, subsidies, government assistance
current_reality.daily_life: Survive on government money, manage poverty, fill time with cheap distractions
extraction_mechanisms: Processed food: Premium paid for cheap ingredients in packaging
current_reality.soul_cost: Judged as 'leech' while actually serving as conduit for parasite subsidy
current_reality.future: Survive on subsidy until death, never escape, always extracted from

the_hidden_truth.you_are_not_the_recipient: Government doesn't subsidize YOU - it subsidizes landlords, insurers, utilities THROUGH you
the_mechanism: Landlord takes $700 (knows you have government money, prices accordingly)
You keep $100 for food, everything else
Result: $1,100 went to parasites, $100 went to you
actual_subsidy_recipients: Landlords receiving guaranteed government money via your rent
Banks extracting fees from those who can't fight back
YOU are the CONDUIT not the recipient
the_hidden_truth.the_scam: They call it 'welfare' and blame you, but parasites get 90% while you get 10% and all the shame

immediate: Rent takes most of subsidy - landlord is real welfare recipient
Poverty trap - lose benefits if work, can't escape
Judgment - called 'leech' while parasites extract through you
Boredom - nothing productive to do, fill time with cheap distractions
Despair - no way out, system designed to keep you trapped

why_discretionary_spending: Boredom - nothing else to do, need distraction from despair
Dopamine - cheap hits from substances, media, games
Escape - life is miserable, need to escape mentally
Social - cigarettes, alcohol enable connection with others in same situation
Rational given constraints - can't save way out of poverty, might as well have small pleasures
spending_reality.society_judges: Spends money on cigarettes/alcohol instead of 'improving self'
spending_reality.reality: After rent-seekers take their cut, $50-$150 won't improve anything - rational to spend on brief pleasure

deeper: Designed to fail - benefits structured to keep you dependent, not help escape
Parasites' feeding ground - you're the mechanism for transferring tax money to rent-seekers
Productive society pays - their taxes fund landlord subsidies via your rent
Everyone loses except parasites - you get scraps, workers' taxes wasted, parasites get rich
Called drain on society while actually being drained BY parasitic society

the_work.philosophy: Everyone can contribute something - find what YOU can do
available_roles: Light labor if able - cleaning, organizing, simple tasks
Childcare support - watch children while parents build
Food preparation - help cook for workers
Community support - talk to others, provide emotional labor
Whatever you CAN do - any contribution valued
the_work.hours: What you can manage - 4 hours? 6 hours? 8 hours? Work to capability
difference_from_current: Actually PRODUCING something - not just existing as parasite conduit
Contributing to sacred mission - building paradise, ending hunger/slaughter
Community values you - not judged as 'leech' but recognized as contributor
Extraction eliminated - your work goes to mission, not to parasites

about_discretionary_spending.the_constraint: No cigarettes, no alcohol, no attention economy, no fireworks in BARRACKS
why_this_exists: Resources commanded toward mission - building paradise, not individual indulgence
Productive workers funding this - their labor shouldn't subsidize waste
Civilizational progress requires focus - distractions prevent mission success
Your own benefit - substances/distractions keep you trapped, removing them helps you grow

why_worth_it: Trading $50/month cigarettes for $3M+/year tokens after 2 years
Trading bored substance use for purposeful contribution
Trading dopamine hits from media for dopamine hits from BUILDING something
Trading escape from misery for elimination of misery's causes
After 2 years: wealthy enough to do whatever you want anyway

current_system_creates_waste: Productive workers: Keep 0.32% of value created (99.68% extracted)
Welfare recipients: Receive $1,200, keep $100 (91.7% extracted to parasites)
Parasites: Extract from both ends (workers' taxes → welfare → rent-seekers = double extraction)
Everyone loses except parasites: Workers poor despite producing, welfare recipients poor despite subsidy
barracks_eliminates_double_extraction: Workers: Keep 100% of contribution (tokens based on work)
Welfare recipients: Keep 100% of contribution (tokens based on work)
Mission: Real cost only ($210/month housing+food vs $1,350/month parasitic cost)
Efficiency: 6.4x cost reduction enables massive scale
the_math: Current: $1,200 subsidy, $1,100 to parasites, $100 to person = 91.7% waste
BARRACKS: $210 real cost, $0 to parasites, person becomes $2M+/year wealthy = 0% waste + prosperity created
Scale: 50M welfare recipients × $1,100/month parasitic extraction = $660B/year wasted
Redirect: $660B to mission instead = builds 50+ global food projects per year

final_message.to_welfare_recipients: You're not the leech - you're the conduit they use to extract from productive workers. Escape by contributing. 2 years hard work, no substances, but $100M+ after. Worth it?
final_message.to_productive_workers: Your taxes subsidize landlords via welfare recipients. Eliminate the parasitism. Put welfare recipients to work building paradise. Your labor to mission not rent-seekers. Worth it?
final_message.to_everyone: Current system extracts from both workers and welfare recipients, enriching parasites. BARRACKS eliminates parasitism, puts everyone to work building paradise, shares wealth fairly. Workers and welfare recipients both winners, parasites eliminated. This is the way.
